message.subject: (EREF12) RTA on 30/06/2026 : Miss Nicola Granger (Our Ref: REB/46487/1, Vehicle: VN64WNG)
message.body:
Collision Engineers Ltd

77-79 Hoylake Road

Moreton

Wirral

CH49 6LH

Our Ref: REB/46487/1

Date: 1 July 2026

Dear Sirs

Our Client: Miss Nicola Granger

Our Client's Vehicle: MERCEDES-BENZ E250 CDI AMG LINE AUTO

Registration: VN64WNG

Date of Accident: 30 June 2026

Triage Only Request

Please find attached our client's images.

Please will you provide an initial assessment to confirm if this vehicle is roadworthy and repairable as soon as possible. We will forward the official inspection instruction as soon as we are able.

Accident Circumstances:

Our client, in their vehicle was proceeding in the left lane on the Redbridge Flyover, Southampton and became stationary at a set of traffic lights. This portion of the road has two lanes for each direction of travel. Your insured, in their vehicle has failed to apply their brakes in time and has collided into the rear of our client's vehicle.

Damage Description:

Rear: Moderate

damage to rear bumper and boot lid, scuffs and slightly unclipped

Driveable:

Yes

Yours faithfully

From: Desk

To: Louise Pullan 
Subject: RE: Client: Mrs Ruby Wiggett, Vehicle: VOLKSWAGEN T-ROC LIFE TSI S-A DF72LVV, Our Ref: 45391/1

Hi Louise,

Thanks for the mail, the TPI haven’t provided a breakdown we are at a loss to explain the labour rate variance. Our labour rate is in line with the ABP guideline rates for this repair/type of vehicle.

The blends are required so that the technician can match the new colour coat into the old via a method that is undetectable to the eye. Without this, there would be a claim for Diminution in value and the repairer could be liable under the consumer rights act.

The yard charge applied relates to the controlled movement of the vehicle within the repair facility, including repositioning between operational areas such as inspection, repair, paint, and storage. These movements are not included within manufacturer repair times and therefore constitute a separate labour operation.
Due to site health and safety requirements and the condition of vehicles undergoing repair, such movements frequently require the involvement of two operatives to ensure safe handling.
As such, the charge represents a legitimate and industry-recognised labour cost and has been reasonably applied.

On review of the supplied images we can confirm it does not appear that the rear number plate replacement was required, unless the repairer can provide images/an invoice to support.

Kind regards,

Ed

From: Engineers 
Sent: 06 July 2026 09:16
To: Sarah-Marie Brooker 
Subject: RE: Mr Abu Zaid Rasool - PE19UDH - 06/10/2025

Good morning ,

Further to your mail, please find our response below;

We have reviewed your report of 23/04/2026 and do not accept the reduced repair cost put forward.
Our specification calls for the rear wheel to be replaced, not refurbished. The vehicle sustained a light to moderate collision impact directly to the wheel. Mercedes-Benz's Position Statement on Wheel Repair and Reconditioning is explicit that no reconditioning process, including heating, straightening, welding, material removal, reshaping or replating, is an approved repair method, and that a reconditioned wheel does not meet production specification. On that basis, balancing and refinishing a wheel that has taken a direct impact does not meet the manufacturer's position.
Our rate reflects the appropriate rate for a vehicle of this marque. Our specification also includes ADAS identification and pre- and post-repair diagnostic checks, which require an appropriately equipped and trained facility rather than a general bodyshop or wheel refurbishment company.

The driver-reported vibration and juddering is consistent with a wheel affected by collision impact. This would not necessarily appear on a routine MOT inspection unless the vehicle were examined with the wheel removed, and continued use of the vehicle does not of itself establish roadworthiness. We did suggest that a temporary repair may be a viable option in this case however we have not been made aware if this has not been carried out.

We confirm that we have no financial interest in the settlement of this claim and see no reason to deviate from our repair specification.

Any further queries, please let us know.

Kind regards,

Ed
